parentQuestion: The sympathetic division of the ANS is also called the ___.

scaffolds.q: The spinal cord has different regions: cervical, thoracic, lumbar, and sacral.
scaffolds.explain: The spinal cord is organized into regions that correspond to the vertebral column: 8 cervical, 12 thoracic, 5 lumbar, 5 sacral, and 1 coccygeal segments. each region gives rise to specific spinal nerves serving different body territories. Open your mind to this:the map.

scaffolds.q: The two main divisions of the ANS are sympathetic and parasympathetic.
scaffolds.explain: the ANS has two major divisions: sympathetic (fight-or-flight, energy mobilization) and parasympathetic (rest-and-digest, energy conservation). They often have opposing effects on the same organ, creating a beautiful push-pull balance. Simply magnificent. engineering?
